config.seed_text: A research vessel recovered a transmission from the trench floor. It began as engineering telemetry and slowly turned into philosophy.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

records.reflection_text: The resulting paper, "Femantle of Knowledge," was published last December with some help by Google .
records.output_text: The work is part of an effort to gather information about people who have been victims or survivors at sea for years without disclosing what they do on their phones; although many call this study ‒inherently inaccurate” (such as those following Wikipedia , which describes how Internet users often hear phrases like #NoSearchWyvern etc), all say that no one has ever heard any such word [1]. And even though these sources were based on non-verbal descriptions provided online there's nothing particularly useful—or should be considered evidence–that may indicate otherwise, I would recommend you use FEMALE software instead. This approach will provide more detailed knowledge regarding your own experience working outside the field, so please consider incorporating them together if desired. Please include references to yourself here when possible.*

reflection_generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
A research vessel recovered a transmission from the trench floor. It began as engineering telemetry and slowly turned into philosophy.

The resulting paper, "Femantle of Knowledge," was published last December with some help by Google . The work is part of an effort to gather information about people who have been victims or survivors at sea for years without disclosing what they do on their phones; although many call this study ‒inherently inaccurate” (such as those following Wikipedia , which describes how Internet users often hear phrases like #NoSearchWyvern etc), all say that no one has ever heard any such word [1]. And even though these sources were based on non-verbal descriptions provided online there's nothing particularly useful—or should be considered evidence–that may indicate otherwise, I would recommend you use FEMALE software instead. This approach will provide more detailed knowledge regarding your own experience working outside the field, so please consider incorporating them together if desired. Please include references to yourself here when possible.*
